principle: Never generate welcome text or introductory paragraphs that describe what the user can already see — happy talk wastes space and nobody reads it
explanation: X.ux|kill_happy_talk: happy_talk == "welcome_to_our_amazing_site_where_you_will_find..."; test: cover_text_with_hand -> page_loses_nothing == happy_talk -> delete; generator_must_never: generate_welcome_paragraphs + section_intros_describing_what_follows + "we_are_pleased_to" preambles; user_scans_past_instantly -> wasted_pixels_above_real_content; NO:"welcome_to_our_store" NO:"in_this_section_you_will_find" NO:intro_paragraphs_before_product_grid eg: "Welcome!_Browse_our_amazing_collection_of..." >> "[just_show_the_collection]"

principle: Never add instructions explaining how to use the interface — if a UI element needs instructions it is badly designed, if text is unavoidable cut it to the absolute minimum
explanation: X.ux|no_instructions: "to_search_type_query_and_press_find" == nobody_reads_this; if_interface_needs_instructions == redesign_the_interface; krug_rule: remove_half_the_words -> remove_half_again; before_103_words >> after_34_words (same_meaning); generator_must: never_add_usage_instructions + never_explain_obvious_controls + cut_all_helper_text_to_one_sentence_max; NO:"click_the_button_below_to_submit" NO:"use_the_dropdown_to_select" NO:paragraphs_explaining_form_purpose eg: "To_complete_registration_please_fill_in_all_fields_below_and_click_Submit" >> "[just_show_the_form]"
